elements.sent-2411-001: Two decades ago the $\chi_{c1}\left(3872\right)$ was discovered in the hadron spectrum with two heavy quarks.
elements.sent-2411-002: The discovery fueled a surge in experimental research, uncovering dozens of so called XYZ exotics states lying outside the conventional quark model, as well as theoretical investigations into new forms of matter, such as quark-gluon hybrids, mesonic molecules, and tetraquarks, with the potential of disclosing new information about the fundamental strong force.
elements.sent-2411-003: Among the XYZs, the $\chi_{c1}\left(3872\right)$ and $T_{cc ^+\left(3875\right)$ stand out for their striking characteristics and unlashed many discussions about their nature.}
elements.sent-2411-004: Here, we address this question using the Born--Oppenheimer Effective Field Theory (BOEFT) and show how QCD settles the issue of their composition.
elements.sent-2411-005: Not only we describe well the main features of the $\chi_{c1}\left(3872\right)$ and $T_{cc ^+\left(3875\right)$ but obtain also model independent predictions in the bottomonium sector.}
elements.sent-2411-006: This opens the way to systematic applications of BOEFT to all XYZs.
elements.sent-2411-007: The discovery \cite{Belle:2003nnu of the $\chi_{c1 (3872)$ (aka X(3872)) state in the charmonium spectrum changed the field of the strong interactions, marking a significant breakthrough.}}
elements.sent-2411-008: A state with $J^{PC = 1^{++}$ and isospin $I=0$, it is remarkable for its mass being within 100 keV of the $D^{*0 \bar {D ^0$ threshold.}}}

elements.sent-2411-010: Its properties suggest that it may be a tetraquark, consisting of four quarks bound together, $Q \bar{Q q \bar{q}$.}
elements.sent-2411-011: Additionally, a plethora of other exotic states, initially termed XYZs, emerged in the sector of the spectrum containing two heavy quarks ($Q\bar{Q}$ or $QQ$) at or above the strong decay thresholds, i.e., the energy above which these states can decay into a pair of heavy-light mesons $M\bar{M}$ \cite{ParticleDataGroup:2024cfk, Brambilla:2019esw .}
elements.sent-2411-012: They include states that are electrically charged, like the $Z_c^{ \pm}$, $Z_b^{\pm}$, and pentaquarks $P$ ($Q \bar{Q q q q$).}

elements.sent-2411-028: BOEFT is a nonrelativistic effective field theory obtained from QCD on the basis of symmetry and scales separation.
elements.sent-2411-029: It is suitable to describe bound states of two heavy quarks and any light degrees of freedom (LDF).
elements.sent-2411-030: Strongly coupled potential NRQCD for quarkonium is the simplest realization of this theory \cite{Brambilla:1999xf,Brambilla:2000gk,Pineda:2000sz,Brambilla:2002nu .}
elements.sent-2411-031: The original BOEFT for hybrids was obtained in~\cite{Berwein:2015vca , with subsequent work~\cite{Brambilla:2017uyf, Oncala:2017hop, Soto:2020xpm, Braaten:2024tbm up to~\cite{Berwein:2024ztx , where the equations for tetraquarks and pentaquarks were derived.}}}
elements.sent-2411-032: The first scale separation exploited by BOEFT is the scale separation between the mass $m_Q$ of the heavy quarks and the energy scale of the LDF, gluons or light quarks part of the binding, which is of the order of the nonperturbative hadronic scale $\Lambda_{\rm QCD}$: $m_Q \gg \Lambda_{\rm QCD}$.
elements.sent-2411-033: QCD static energies for all LDF excitations in the presence of two quarks separated by a distance $r$ are classified in terms of the {\it BO quantum numbers .}
